title: Deuterium
text: Home * Engines * Deuterium Deuterium discharge tube [1] Deuterium , an UCI compatible chess engine by Ferdinand Mosca , initially based on TSCP or Tom's Simple Chess Program by Tom Kerrigan . With version 14.2.33.276, released in June 2014, the former Chess Engine Communication Protocol compliance was superseded by UCI [2] . Contents 1 Features 1.1 Board Representation 1.2 Search 1.3 Evaluation 1.4 Misc 2 Tournament Play 3 Selected Games 4 Forum Posts 4.1 2009 4.2 2010 ... 4.3 2015 ... 4.4 2020 ... 5 External Links 5.1 Chess Engine 5.2 Misc 6 References Features [ edit ] [3] Board Representation [ edit ] 8x8 Board Piece-Lists Search [ edit ] Iterative Deepening Aspiration Windows Fail-Soft NegaScout Transposition Table Selectivity Null Move Pruning Move History/Statistics Pruning Futility Pruning Late Move Reductions Razoring Move Ordering Static Exchange Evaluation Killer Heuristic History Heuristic Countermove Heuristic Evaluation [ edit ] Material Evaluation Hash Table Piece-Square Tables Pawn Structure Pawn Hash Table Outposts Mobility King Safety Automated Tuning Misc [ edit ] PolyGlot Opening Book 6-men Scorpio Bitbases Tournament Play [ edit ] Deuterium participated at most ACCA World Computer Rapid Chess Championships , first in 2007 , as well as most recent CCT Tournaments since CCT11 . Over the board, Deuterium became third at the CCC 2009 4th Chess Computer Cup in Carugate , Italy , where it also played the 1st Italian Open Chess Software Cup in 2010.
